commit.message:
Fix false positives in exploit/scan modules (FW3.7.1)

- Add _discover_params(): crawl real page params before testing
- SQLi: use baseline comparison to filter pre-existing SQL-like text
- SQLi: time-based threshold now requires +4s vs baseline (not raw 2.5s)
- XSS: use unique session marker instead of generic payloads
- XSS: remove DOM sink false positives (innerHTML/eval exist everywhere)
- CMDi: use echo CMDI_CONFIRMED marker, remove generic 'ttl' indicator
- LFI: remove empty indicator payload (matched everything)
- LFI: use specific indicators (root:x:0:0:, HTTP_HOST=)
- vBulletin: require 'vbulletin' in response body (not just 302)
- upload_check: only report 200 with upload form content (not 403)
- sqli_scan: discover real params from page before injecting

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

docs: Add Remote Function rationale document (#26)

* feat: Context Graph V2 — Property Graph, AI.GENERATE extraction, World Change detection (#21)

- Add context_graph.py module: ContextGraphManager with BizNode extraction
  via AI.GENERATE (full Vertex AI endpoint URL for Gemini 3.x+), Property
  Graph DDL with LABEL/KEY following BQ Studio patterns, GQL reasoning
  chain traversal with TO_JSON returns, and World Change drift detection
- Add client.context_graph() factory method
- Export BizNode, ContextGraphConfig, ContextGraphManager, WorldChangeAlert,
  WorldChangeReport from __init__.py
- Add 30 unit tests for all context graph functionality
- Add context_graph_adcp_demo.ipynb: 12-phase customer-facing demo with
  ADCP multi-agent media buying, trace retrieval, entity extraction,
  Property Graph DDL, GQL visualization cells for BQ Studio, world change
  detection, full evaluation pipeline, multi-trial, and AI insights
- Notebook executed end-to-end with gemini-3-flash-preview model

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: Address P1-P4 review findings for context graph correctness

P1: Use composite keys to prevent collisions/over-merging
  - BizNode keyed by biz_node_id (span_id:node_type:node_value)
  - Evaluated edges keyed by link_id (span_id:node_value)
  - Cross-links table includes biz_node_id for proper referencing

P2: Parameterize biz_entity via @biz_entity query parameter
  - Eliminates SQL injection / query-break risk from entity values
  - explain_decision() conditionally adds the parameter

P3: Make extraction pipeline idempotent
  - AI.GENERATE now uses MERGE upsert (not CREATE OR REPLACE TABLE)
  - create_cross_links() deletes existing session links before insert
  - Re-running for subset of sessions no longer wipes other data

P4: Fix misleading docstring for detect_world_changes
  - Docs now correctly state that current_state_fn=None returns a safe
    report with entity count (no simulated check)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: Address 4 issue-specific findings for Context Graph (#21)

1. Add output_schema to AI.GENERATE extraction SQL for strict
   structured output per issue Step 2
2. Add GQL-based trace reconstruction via reconstruct_trace_gql()
   and Client.get_session_trace_gql() per issue Step 4
3. Model Persisted Artifacts pillar: artifact_uri on BizNode,
   cross-links table, and Evaluated edge properties in DDL
4. Use _WORLD_CHANGE_CHECK_QUERY with evaluated_at timestamp in
   detect_world_changes() for temporal drift semantics

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: Edge-key collision, stale biz node cleanup, GQL fallback

1. Derive cross-link link_id from biz_node_id (includes node_type)
   to prevent collision when same span has entities with same
   node_value but different node_type
2. Add WHEN NOT MATCHED BY SOURCE ... DELETE to MERGE so stale
   biz nodes for target sessions are removed on re-extraction
3. get_session_trace_gql falls back to flat SQL query when GQL
   returns no edges (sparse/flat traces) instead of raising

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: Merge isolated events in GQL trace, return artifact_uri from read APIs

1. GQL trace reconstruction now merges with flat SQL query to
   capture isolated events (no Caused edges) that would otherwise
   be silently dropped in mixed sessions
2. artifact_uri is now selected and mapped in _read_biz_nodes,
   get_biz_nodes_for_session, and _BIZ_NODES_FOR_SESSION_QUERY
   so callers see the persisted artifact linkage

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: Parent linkage backfill, span ordering, artifact_uri in store, delete error handling

1. Backfill parent_span_id when a span is first seen as parent_
   then later as child_ in GQL rows (prevents wrong tree structure)
2. Sort final spans by (timestamp, span_id) for deterministic
   chronological order
3. Include artifact_uri in store_biz_nodes insert payload
4. Cross-link delete now only suppresses "not found" errors;
   real failures (permissions, etc.) propagate as return False

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: datetime import, legacy endpoint rejection, fail-closed world-change

1. Import datetime/timezone in client.py and use timezone-aware
   epoch fallback for span sorting (fixes F821 runtime crash)
2. _resolve_endpoint() rejects legacy BQ ML model refs
   (project.dataset.model) with clear ValueError
3. detect_world_changes() is now fail-closed: query failures
   return check_failed=True, is_safe_to_approve=False instead
   of silently reporting "safe"

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: Fail-closed on current_state_fn errors + clean lint debt

- Track callback failures in detect_world_changes(); set check_failed=True
  and is_safe_to_approve=False when any current_state_fn call throws
- Remove unused imports (asyncio, json) from context_graph.py
- Remove unused local variable in test_extract_query_has_output_schema
- Remove unused Span import in test_get_session_trace_gql_chronological_order
- Update test_detect_world_changes_fn_exception to expect fail-closed behavior

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* docs: Add Context Graph documentation across README, SDK.md, and design.md

- README.md: Add Context Graph to features list and architecture tree
- SDK.md: Add Section 17 with full Context Graph API reference covering
  ContextGraphManager, BizNode extraction, GQL trace reconstruction,
  world-change detection with fail-closed semantics, and data models;
  update module architecture and dependency graph
- docs/design.md: Add context_graph.py to architecture diagram, module
  categorization table, module design (Section 4.14), SDK-created tables,
  Pydantic data models, and future directions (14.6 implemented, 14.7 evolution)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* docs: Add Context Graph V2 design document

Comprehensive design doc for the Context Graph V2 demo covering:
- 4-Pillar Property Graph architecture (TechNode + BizNode + Caused + Evaluated)
- AI.GENERATE with output_schema for structured entity extraction
- GQL reasoning chains and trace reconstruction
- World-change detection with fail-closed semantics
- MERGE with DELETE for stale BizNode cleanup
- Data model (agent_events, biz_nodes, cross_links)
- Production query patterns (5 SQL/GQL templates)
- Interactive demo features and deployment
- Evolution from V1 to V2

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* docs: Add concise Remote Function rationale document

Distills the full PRD (prd_unified_analytics_interface.md) into a
focused <5-page document explaining why the SDK needs a BigQuery
Remote Function interface. Covers:

- The problem: SQL analysts locked out of SDK capabilities
- Solution: single multiplexed agent_analytics() remote function
- 3 concrete user journeys (dashboard, LLM judge, drift alert)
- Deployment steps and least-privilege IAM
- Cost model and performance targets
- Remote Function vs Continuous Query comparison

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix font override: set --font-serif and --font-lora to Mercury SSm in klug template

Components use Tailwind font-serif class which resolves to --font-lora,
overriding the .klug-template h1 CSS rules. Fix by overriding the CSS
variables at the template level.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Use longest dwell cluster for conflict resolution time prefill

Previously the start/end times were prefilled from the first/last nearby
ping within 150m of the property for the entire day. A truck passing by
later (e.g. heading home) would stretch the end time far past the actual
service window. Now the JS dwell cluster detection (which groups
consecutive stationary pings) updates the form fields with the longest
cluster's time range — the "likely service" window.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
UAT: Security hardening, testing, and CI quality gates (US-01 through US-11)

Wave 1 — Critical fixes:
- US-01: Remove leaked PII and hardcoded infrastructure URLs
- US-02: Fix getBaseApiPath double-prefix bug and hardcoded tool count
- US-03: Add entity name validation to all CRUD methods
- US-04: Harden OAuth — JWT audience validation, CSRF state, JWT detection
- US-05: Harden Copilot SSE — session cache, input validation, sanitization

Wave 2 — Reliability:
- US-06: Fix session memory leak with LRU cache, DCR timing-safe comparison
- US-07: Replace all console.* with structured logger
- US-10: Harden HTTP middleware — block CORS wildcard, sanitize request IDs

Wave 3 — Quality:
- US-08: Add testing infrastructure with Vitest (110 tests, 5 suites)
- US-09: Fix OData validator false positives and type coercion bugs
- US-11: Add CI quality gates and pre-publish checks

Additional:
- Default TOOL_MODE to generic (opt-out via TOOL_MODE=dynamic)
- Pin GitHub Actions to SHA for supply chain security
- Install missing ESLint plugins, make config realistic (0 errors)
- Scope security audit to production dependencies only

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Resolve symlinks in SCRIPT_DIR for installed symlink to work

When installed via symlink (/usr/local/bin -> /usr/local/lib/hermes-fly),
BASH_SOURCE points to the symlink, making SCRIPT_DIR wrong. Follow
symlink chain with readlink to find the real file location.

Fix banner stats: remove parentheses from IN clause for clickhouse-go v2

The clickhouse-go v2 driver requires `IN ?` (no parentheses) when
binding Go slices. `IN (?)` was silently failing, causing all banner
stats to show 0. Also added error logging.

feat: add OTLP forwarding/proxy mode

Add --forward-to flag to `motel server` that forwards all incoming OTLP
data (traces, logs, metrics) to one or more upstream endpoints while
still storing it locally. This makes motel useful as a development
sidecar — see data locally in the TUI while also sending it to
production observability backends.

- Add --forward-to, --forward-headers, --forward-timeout CLI args
- Create OtlpForwarder in src/server/forwarder.rs with fire-and-forget
  async forwarding (tokio::spawn per endpoint)
- Integrate forwarder into both gRPC and HTTP ingestion paths
- Forward failures are logged but never block ingestion
- Support multiple --forward-to flags for fan-out
- Support custom headers via --forward-headers for API keys
- Add unit tests for forwarder construction and header parsing
- Add integration tests: two-server forwarding and dead endpoint resilience

Closes #12

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: Dover 403 + micro1 timeout

Dover: drop SCRAPE_HEADERS (browser fingerprint) from all three Dover
fetch calls. Cloudflare's bot scoring flags mismatched TLS fingerprints
(Cloudflare Workers) + browser-style headers; using plain Accept-only
headers avoids the heuristic mismatch and the 403.

micro1: raise LIMIT from 18 → 100 (fewer page round-trips) and add a
MAX_PAGES = 5 hard cap. Both changes bound worst-case fetch time and
prevent the 20 s per-company ceiling from being hit when micro1 has
many contractor marketplace listings to paginate through.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Fix booking API: use update for existing bookings, trim APP_URL newline

- Root cause: NEXT_PUBLIC_APP_URL env var has trailing \n, causing Stripe
  to reject checkout session URLs as invalid
- Added .trim() to appUrl to handle whitespace/newlines in env vars
- Replaced delete+insert with update for existing pending bookings
  (RLS has no DELETE policy on bookings table)
- Added fallback to request.url origin if APP_URL is missing
- Better error messages showing actual failure reason

commit.message:
Start web UI immediately, fetch M3U in background

fetch_m3u() was running synchronously before app.run(), blocking the
web UI for 20+ seconds while downloading and parsing 16K+ M3U entries.
Now fetches in a background thread so the dashboard is available instantly.
Saved channels are still restored synchronously for immediate use.

commit.message:
feat: quality sweep, new features, dashboard cards, and multi-round review fixes

Quality sweep:
- Centralize env vars in Zod schema (redaction, upload limits, webhook config)
- Add 49 new tests (prompt-lint, rate-limit, redact, upload-postprocess)
- Fix export empty result edge case, extract TOKEN_CHAR_RATIO constant
- Replace console.warn with logger, make hardcoded limits configurable

New features:
- CLI: Gemini hook support, omp ask/analyze/report commands
- Backend: webhook events (prompt.enriched, session.completed), redaction consistency
- Backend: quality dimensions (clarity, specificity, context, constraints, structure)
- Backend: search filters (qualityScore, topicTags), prompt linting (6 rules)
- Dashboard: token usage, quality score, and topics cards with charts
- Learning metrics: weekly reports with vocabulary diversity and structure usage

Review fixes (R7-R9):
- Fix SQL cast for topicTags (::text[]), quality dims condition, date validation
- Fix auth redirects (redirect instead of null), SVG gradient unique ID
- Fix N+1 learning.trend query (104 → 1 DB call via batch function)
- Add DB indexes (userId+qualityScore, userId+sessionId+timestamp)
- Add CASCADE/SET NULL constraints on sharedPrompts/allowedEmails
- Standardize Pino logging ({ err }), null project defaults ("Unknown")
- Fix missing path import in ingest.js (crashed omp ingest --replay)
- Fix reversed session timestamps on detail page (wrong duration)
- Fix SESSION_SECRET: use validated env.SESSION_SECRET, not process.env
- Fix learning metrics OOM: truncate promptText to LEFT(500)
- Merge dashboard 13 queries into single Promise.all batch
- Fix --json flag collision, add WebhookEvent type safety
- Fix similarity() queries to match trigram index expression
- Fix weekKeyForDate to use UTC methods

commit.message:
Scanner: 20 concurrent scanners + crash fixes

Crash fixes:
- queueSize variabele was block-scoped maar werd buiten try-block
  gebruikt → ReferenceError crash. Nu isLargeQueue vóór try gedeclareerd
- Lightweight mode laadde image 2x (resizeForApi + loadImage apart),
  nu 1x laden en hergebruiken voor beide outputs
- activeCountRef kon negatief worden of ghost slots hebben:
  - Alle decrements gebruiken nu Math.max(0, ...)
  - Queue processor synct activeCountRef met werkelijke scanning count
  - Stale detector en processItem finally block coördineren proper
- Stale detector zet nu naar 'error' (niet 'waiting') om retry-storms
  te voorkomen, en decrement activeCountRef zelf

Concurrency:
- 20 concurrent scanners op alle platforms (was 4-8 op mobiel)
- Browser limiteert HTTP connecties tot 6/host automatisch
- Lightweight mode (50+ items) houdt geheugen per scan op ~200KB

Auto-retry:
- Exponential backoff (5s, 15s, 30s, 60s) i.p.v. elke 8s
- Max 5 retries tegelijk i.p.v. alle errors tegelijk
- 'Geheugen vol' errors worden niet meer geretried

commit.message:
Add parquet-based OHLCV cache with TTL and merge

- Parquet files with .meta.json sidecar for TTL tracking
- Merge strategy: union of timestamps, newer data wins on conflict
- Invalidation by symbol/timeframe/provider, cleanup of expired entries
- Handles corrupt files gracefully (auto-delete and return None)
- 12 tests covering round-trip, TTL, merge, sanitization, corruption

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

research: formalize Ehrhart polynomials (picks-theorem-oq-03) (#3363)

* research: prove simply_connected_of_homeomorphic (eliminate axiom)

Convert the axiom `simply_connected_of_homeomorphic` to a proved theorem using
Mathlib's FundamentalGroupoidFunctor.equivOfHomotopyEquiv. The proof composes
the fundamental groupoid equivalence from a homeomorphism (via homotopy equiv)
with the SimplyConnectedSpace equivalence to Discrete Unit.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* research: formalize Ehrhart polynomials (picks-theorem-oq-03)

19 proved theorems, 11 axioms, 0 sorries. Ehrhart polynomial
theory as the correct higher-dimensional generalization of Pick's
theorem, with Reeve tetrahedra counterexample showing Pick's
formula cannot extend to 3D.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat!: migrate event lifecycle to Spring State Machine

Replace 5 independent scheduled processors with a single Spring State
Machine that makes the event lifecycle explicit:
PLANNED → NOTIFIED → ALBUM_POSTED → COMPLETED → ARCHIVED → DELETED

- Expand EventState enum with NOTIFIED, ALBUM_POSTED, COMPLETED states
- Add state machine config, guards, actions, service, and unified poller
- Rename attendanceLocked to completed in API and frontend
- Add Liquibase migration to map existing events to new states
- Add index on event.state column for poller queries
- Remove spring-boot-starter-batch dependency
- Delete all old processor classes and their tests
- Add new guard, action, and poller tests

BREAKING CHANGE: EventDetailDto field renamed from attendanceLocked to
completed. Frontend consumers must update accordingly.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Rework §1.5: build matrices 2x2 → 3x3 → general N

- Remove direct jump to NxN matrix
- Step 1: N=2 matrices written out, multiply verified for i and v
- Step 2: N=3 matrices, multiply verified for all 3 outputs
- physbox: pattern comparison — DM rows have +1/2,-1/2 on adjacent
  columns; CM row is all-ones (Ti) or all-1/N (Tv); growing N just
  adds one DM row
- Step 3: general NxN equations (5)-(8) now feel self-evident
- §1.6 renamed to "What the Inverse Gives You", retains N=2 inverse
  eqnbox and voltage explanation

fix: render repo-file shortcode as HTML anchor instead of raw markdown

The `{{< >}}` shortcode syntax in Hugo does not process its output as
markdown, so the `[text](url)` link syntax was appearing as literal
text in the rendered page. Switch to outputting an `<a><code>` tag
directly.

Fixes broken links on all example pages (e.g., datacenter-cost "Try It").

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add idempotent imports with dedup manifests and entry-level merge

Source-level dedup: hash source file before import, check existing
manifests. If same file was already imported, skip with message.
--force overrides.

Entry-level merge: when writing to a day that already has entries
from the same source, compute content keys and skip duplicates,
appending only new entries.

- hash_source() for file and directory SHA-256
- write_manifest() / find_manifest_by_hash() for manifest lifecycle
- write_structured_import() merges with existing JSONL on re-import
- CLI checks source hash before processing, writes manifest after
- 14 new tests for hashing, manifests, entry merge, and dedup
- Updated 4 existing tests for manifest expectations

orphaned tables with missing DEFAULT clauses. INSERT INTO

but the stale table lacks that DEFAULT, producing NULL id violations.

and dq_schema.sql before their CREATE TABLE statements.
